features.title: Global card acceptance network
features.description: Enables transactions across a large network of merchants, issuers, and acquirers using Visa-branded cards.

features.title: Card-present and card-not-present payments
features.description: Supports in-person payments (including EMV chip and contactless) and online/remote card payments.

features.title: Tokenization support
features.description: Provides tokenization services (e.g., network tokens) to help reduce exposure of primary account numbers in digital payments.

features.title: Dispute and chargeback processes
features.description: Includes rules and processes for disputes, chargebacks, and reversals handled through participating issuers and acquirers.

features.title: Security and fraud tooling
features.description: Offers network-level security programs and related services that work with issuer/acquirer systems to help manage fraud risk.

features.title: Cross-border processing
features.description: Facilitates international transactions and currency conversion workflows through participating financial institutions.

seo_title: Visa payment network: cards, tokenization, and acceptance
seo_description: Visa is a global card payment network that connects cardholders, merchants, acquirers, and issuers to authorize and settle electronic payments using Visa-brands
keywords: Visa, card payments, payment network, credit card, debit card, tokenization, EMV, VisaNet
excerpt: Visa is a global payment network used for card-based transactions and related services such as tokenization and card-present/card-not-resent

availability.note: Visa acceptance varies by country and by issuer/acquirer participation.

tip: When integrating card payments, Visa is typically supported via a payment processor or acquiring bank rather than directly by most merchants.

faq.question: Is Visa a bank or a payment processor?
faq.answer: Visa is a payment network. Most merchants connect to Visa through a payment processor or acquiring bank that provides the merchant account and gateway services.

faq.question: Can a merchant integrate directly with Visa?
faq.answer: Direct integration is uncommon for most merchants; typically you work with a processor/acquirer that connects to Visa. Large organizations may have direct relationships depending on region and requirements.

faq.question: What is the difference between Visa and a Visa card issuer?
faq.answer: Visa provides the network rails and rules. The issuer (usually a bank or fintech) provides the card to the cardholder and manages the account and credit/debit relationship.

faq.question: Does Visa handle chargebacks?
faq.answer: Chargebacks are initiated and managed by the issuer and acquirer under Visa network rules; Visa provides the framework and network messaging for the process.
